title: So the real question why was Shinji given a choice
display_title: So the real question why was Shinji given a choice
wikitext:
[]: So the real question: why was Shinji given a choice?             Quote Post by persepolisliesinruins » Wed Dec 14, 2011 6:13 am Was the choice to not MERGE into a single entity available from the beginning to all human beings, or given them after Shinji decided he didn't want to? And if so, why did Lilith allow him (and them) to choose? I see Rei as a Christ figure, a mean the divinity has to experience the life of his/her descendants-lesser beings. Of course, the Christian God is a trascending and overpowering one, while Lilith is an immanent and not that almighty, but being hosted (by choice or by incident) into a human body is a way for them to LEARN, EXPERIENCE, and in the end, EVOLVE. I like to think (but I might be wrong, and this topic is just to clarify it) that the time spent in a human body with a human mind (despite her "divine" soul) as Rei has drivenLilith to understand what human beings really are, despite the physical and psychic barriers that dived them, or maybe just because of such frontiers, and then decide to give them the chance to choice for thmeselves. Or maybe I shoul just have another drink :)

[]: "Why was Shinji given a choice?" - Depends. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Dec 14, 2011 11:15 am Are you asking in terms of the series, or EoE? In EoE, he was the one sitting in EVA-01, which was the instrument for making 3rd impact possible. Further, Rei initiated her part in an act to comfort Shinji, so it makes sense she would give him this choice. If you're talking of just the series by itself, that's a bit more obscure, and some here even like to suggest other people DID decide their fate for themselves, and the only person Shinji was deciding for was himself. There are indications he's in the one in full control in the series, and EoE itself supports that notion, clearly, but I'm saying that there is room to interpret it both ways. But presuming he is the one with the "total" choice in the series, the best reasoning I can give you is that he's the protagonist, and as such, he's the focal point, and that the narrative decidedly painted him into a corner to force him to make hard choices to better himself. And who knows, it may have even been a reference to the Sartrean concept of anguish over our condemnation to be free; " In choosing for ourselves, we choose for all humankind. " Though given Hideaki Anno's own admission of "Did not do the research", that's a tentative point at best.

[]: Re: "Why was Shinji given a choice?" - Depends. Bl             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Dec 14, 2011 12:07 pm Alaska Slim wrote: If you're talking of just the series by itself, that's a bit more obscure, and some here even like to suggest other people DID decide their fate for themselves, and the only person Shinji was deciding for was himself.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 14, 2011 3:43 pm Rei was the one given control over everything, and she passed the buck to Shinji. If he chose Instrumentality, everyone would have merged. He rejected it, so everyone is separate, but everyone still has to choose to re-embody.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Wed Dec 14, 2011 6:41 pm I prefer the notion that every single character had a choice, of ether staying in the forced unification of instrumentality or forging ahead as separate entities with their own sense of identity, at least in the series . However in The End of Evangelion Rei/Lilith gives Shinji the choice alone.

[]:             Quote Post by Damaijin » Wed Dec 14, 2011 10:59 pm In "Knocking on Heaven's Door", I remember Kiel (or someone else in Seele) saying that mankind is ever to change, he has to make his own decisions--so that might be why a human had to chose. I also think there's an article on the wiki somewhere (I can't remember where) about a real world concept describing how mankind can only evolve so far, and that the next step for them to fully ascend would require the assistance of a higher/deity-like lifeform. In terms of Evangelion, it would be Lillith. Now, as to why she picked Shinji, I think it had to do with what took place in Terminal Dogma. She heard Kaworu talking about how humans deserved to live, and I think her seeing Shinji's pain and turmoil over having to kill someone he cared about and who honestly cared about him also contributed to her choosing him specifically. I think that, in her eyes, if anyone deserved a chance at solace, it was this sad little broken progeny of hers (plus, she really didn't seem to be pleased with Gendo, for whatever reasons). Anyway, this is just my impression.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Dec 15, 2011 2:07 am Damaijin wrote: Now, as to why she picked Shinji, I think it had to do with what took place in Terminal Dogma. She heard Kaworu talking about how humans deserved to live, and I think her seeing Shinji's pain and turmoil over having to kill someone he cared about and who honestly cared about him also contributed to her choosing him specifically. I think that, in her eyes, if anyone deserved a chance at solace, it was this sad little broken progeny of hers.

[]:             Quote Post by Damaijin » Sat Dec 31, 2011 11:24 pm CJD wrote: That's something that never crossed my mind. I'm not entirely convinced, but that's a very intriguing thought, what Lilith was thinking during that whole scene.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Jan 01, 2012 12:44 pm No need to quote the entire previous post Interesting read, but I rather meant "What was going through the Stay Puft Marshmallow Woman's head?". What was the big white blob of Lilith thinking during the entire scene? Was she thinking? Was she capable of thought after having atleast some of her soul taken from her, possibly all? We see her move around before being reunited with Rei, so she's presumably conscious. As for the rest, I don't know enough about Rei to really understand her beyond the most basics, so I won't pretend to comment on the validity of what you said, because I don't know.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 01, 2012 1:06 pm @topic: Because Rei's a bitch. The more charitable reading: It's possible Lilith passed the buck to Shinji because, being a SoL, she might not have the right to decide upon a species' fate once she's given it life. Her function is to create, not to govern. Were she an ordinary human she'd realize dumping things in Shinji's lap was a terrible idea, but since she is who she is and things played out as they did her only choice was who got to make the decision, not whether or not it was made. SEELE? Gendo? Shinji? The choice is kind of obvious in that light.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jan 01, 2012 4:56 pm In EoE? Because he was the only one who gave a shit about her as a person. She probably was also drawn to participate in the ritual already in progress the same way the spear just came flying down out of nowhere when things were ready for it. In EoTV? Everybody had a choice, Shinji was taking longer than most.
